title: ceriops timoriensis
intro: Ceriops timoriensis, also known as the black mangrove or bakau hitam, is a species of mangrove tree native to Southeast Asia and the Pacific Ocean. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with traditional uses including the treatment of fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues. The health benefits of Ceriops timoriensis include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce swelling and prevent infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark is often used to make decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
